While there were some elaborations added about broader implications of managing neurological waste, they do not alter our core conclusion about ammonia removal mechanism. Specifically, glutamine acts as a carrier for amino groups across the blood-brain barrier, facilitating safe disposal of ammonia once it leaves neural tissue. This makes it distinctively suited compared to alanine, which mainly deals with amino acid balance adjustments locally within muscles and kidneys without direct involvement in interorgan transportation of ammonia-derived nitrogen species.
